attacking_style: Pragmatic opportunist who prioritizes time bonuses and GC gains over stage wins. Attacks strategically for podium positions that maximize time gaps rather than always contesting victories.
team_tactics: Relies on strong positional discipline in technical finales rather than heavy domestique support. Team setup appears minimal, with Lutsenko executing individually in critical moments.
terrain_preference: Technical circuit finishes and time trial-influenced stages where power and bike handling combine. Thrives in scenarios requiring tactical positioning over pure climbing.
vulnerability: Limited data, but profile suggests pure mountain-top finishes against specialized climbers may expose limitations if unable to gain time through bonuses or TT segments.
key_patterns: Prioritizes time bonuses strategically, taking third for bonus seconds over risky win attempts (2 observations)
Translates time-trial strength into race-winning defensive riding and positioning (2 observations)
Demonstrates mature GC calculation, defending small time gaps through technical finishes (2 observations)
Prefers tactical placement over raw aggression in stage finales (2 observations)
form_trajectory: unknown: 3-year gap between observations makes recent form impossible to assess reliably
